text.title: Agent Eval Test-Set Curation Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example applying agent-eval-test-set-curation to a representative case. Walks step-by-step through the procedure, shows the produced artefact, and highlights the rules it satisfies.

section.title: Setup
section.p: Context: a team that matches the Applies-If conditions for agent-eval-test-set-curation. Inputs are populated per the Prerequisites table.

list.item: Step 1: Define capability + persona + difficulty taxonomies. — produced tags.md.
Step 2: Sample ≥30% from production traces using stratified sampling. — produced real-set.jsonl.
Step 3: Hand-author or synthesise remaining cases to ≥30 total. — produced golden.jsonl draft.
Step 4: Tag every case with capability + persona + difficulty + provenance. — produced tagged golden.jsonl.
Step 5: Partition into fast-subset (≤20%) + full-suite. — produced partitioned set.
Step 6: Commit `evals/golden.jsonl`; document refresh cadence. — produced versioned set.

section.title: Final artefact
section.p: The team commits the final artefact (per spec shape) at the version-controlled path declared in the output contract. Owner signs and links it to downstream consumers.

list.item: r1: Golden set has ≥30 trajectories; ≥30% sourced from real production traces (not synthetic).
r2: Each trajectory has ≥3 tags: capability (e.g., 'tool-use', 'retrieval'), persona (e.g., 'novice
r3: Every real-trace-sourced trajectory carries a `provenance` field with trace ID, sampling rule, capture date.
r4: Golden set is version-controlled at `evals/golden.jsonl`; changes go through PR with reviewer.
r5: Set is partitioned: fast-subset tag for ≤20% of cases (stratified across capabilities); full-suite is the remainder.
